common.tagline: Agentic RAG with clickable citations and synced document viewer.

brandPanel.title: Answers you can defend.
brandPanel.body: Cite turns your documents into grounded answers — every claim linked to the exact passage it came from. Click any citation and see the source, highlighted.

login.title: Sign in to Cite
login.subtitle: Welcome back.
login.errorBody: Check your details and try again, or reset your password.

signup.title: Create your Cite account
signup.subtitle: Set up your workspace and start chatting with your documents.
signup.passwordHint: Password must be at least 8 characters.

checkEmail.title: Check your email
checkEmail.body: We sent a confirmation link to {email}. Click it to activate your account.
checkEmail.bodyGeneric: We sent a confirmation link to your inbox. Click it to activate your account.
checkEmail.spamHint: Didn't get it? Check spam, or

forgotPassword.title: Reset your password
forgotPassword.subtitle: Enter your email and we'll send a link to set a new password.

resetSent.title: Check your email
resetSent.body: If an account exists for {email}, we sent a link to reset your password.
resetSent.bodyGeneric: If an account exists for that address, we sent a link to reset your password.
resetSent.spamHint: Didn't get it? Check spam, or

setPassword.title: Set a new password
setPassword.passwordHint: Password must be at least 8 characters.
setPassword.expiredBody: For your security, reset links are valid for one hour. Request a new one to continue.

acceptInvite.switchNote: Accepting switches your active workspace to {org}. You can switch back anytime.
acceptInvite.expiredBody: This link is no longer valid. Ask an admin to send a new invitation.
acceptInvite.notFoundBody: It may have been revoked or already used. Ask an admin for a fresh link.
acceptInvite.mismatchTitle: This invite is for a different email

createOrg.title: Create your organization
createOrg.subtitle: Workspaces in Cite are organized by org. Pick a name your team will recognize.

selectOrg.title: Choose a workspace
selectOrg.subtitle: You belong to several. Pick one to continue.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.emptyBody: Upload a document to get started.

onboarding.title: Your workspace is ready
onboarding.body: Cite turns your documents into answers you can defend. Add sources, then ask questions and get responses grounded in exact, verifiable passages.
onboarding.step1Body: Group related documents — one matter, product, or policy set.
onboarding.step2Body: PDF, DOCX, HTML or Markdown — up to 100 MB each.
onboarding.step3Body: Every answer arrives with inline, clickable citations.

organization.title: Organization
organization.description: Workspace-level information visible to everyone in this organization.
organization.adminHint: Admins manage members and view citation accuracy.
organization.readOnlyHint: Only admins can edit workspace details.

preferences.title: Preferences
preferences.description: Personal settings for your account. These follow you across devices.
preferences.languageHint: Affects the UI and messages app-wide.

members.permissionDeniedDescription: Only workspace admins can manage members and invites. Ask an admin if you need access.

invite.title: Invite a teammate
invite.description: Generate a one-time link. Add an email to pin it to that address, or share an open link.
invite.copyFailed: Couldn't copy. Select the link and copy it manually.

removeDialog.title: Remove {name}?
removeDialog.description: They lose access to this workspace immediately. Their comments remain.

revokeDialog.title: Revoke this invite?
revokeDialog.description: The link stops working right away and can't be restored.

lastAdmin.title: You can't remove the last admin
lastAdmin.description: Promote another member to admin before removing or demoting yourself.

noneYet.title: No conversations yet
noneYet.description: Start one to ask grounded questions about a collection — every answer comes back with verifiable citations.

noResults.title: No conversations match
noResults.description: Nothing for "{query}". Try a different search or clear the filter.
noResults.descriptionFiltered: No conversations in this collection yet. Try a different filter.

noCollections.title: Create a collection first
noCollections.description: You need at least one collection with indexed documents before you can start a conversation.

dialog.title: New conversation
dialog.description: Choose a collection to ground your answers in.

documentViewer.failedDescription: Something went wrong fetching this source. Check your connection and try again.
documentViewer.notFoundDescription: This source may have been deleted or moved out of the collection. The citation can't be opened.
documentViewer.unsupportedDescription: The cited text is still highlighted below. Download the original to view it with full formatting.

errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
errors.unauthorized: You don't have access to this page.

audit.title: Citation accuracy
audit.subtitle: LLM-judge verdicts on every citation across recent assistant messages.

empty.title: No audited messages yet
empty.description: Every citation is scored automatically after an answer is generated. Once your team starts chatting with sources, accuracy results appear here.

filteredEmpty.title: No matching citations
filteredEmpty.description: No audited citations match the current filters. Try clearing the search or verdict filter.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.description: The page you're looking for doesn't exist or may have moved.

error.title: Something went wrong
error.description: An unexpected error occurred and our team has been notified. Try reloading — your work is saved.

forbidden.title: You don't have access
forbidden.description: This page is restricted to workspace admins. Ask an admin if you think you should have access.

offline.title: You're offline
offline.description: Check your connection. Cite will reconnect automatically and pick up where you left off.

removed.title: You've been removed from this workspace
removed.description: An admin removed your access to this workspace. You can still use any other workspaces you belong to.

documents.title: Documents
documents.documentCount: {count, plural, =0 {No documents} one {# document} other {# documents}}

deleteDocument.title: Delete "{name}"?
deleteDocument.description: This removes the document and any citations that reference it from this collection. This can't be undone.

deleteCollection.title: Delete "{name}"?
deleteCollection.description: This deletes the collection and all {count, plural, one {# document} other {# documents}} in it. This can't be undone.

form.name: Name
form.descriptionHint: Group related documents to chat with together.

upload2.tooLarge: Too large — {size}. Files must be under {max}.
upload2.unsupported: Unsupported format. Cite accepts PDF, DOCX, HTML and Markdown.
upload2.noCollection: No collection selected. Choose or create a collection before uploading.

empty.noCollectionsBody: Collections group related documents so you can chat with them together. Create your first to start uploading sources.
empty.noSelectionBody: Choose a collection on the left to see and manage its documents.
empty.emptyCollectionBody: Drag files here or browse to upload. PDF, DOCX, HTML, Markdown — up to {size} each.

hero.title: Every answer, traced to the source.
hero.subtitle: Cite turns your documents into grounded answers — every claim linked to the exact passage it came from. Click a citation, see the highlight.
hero.note: 100% free · works with PDF, DOCX, HTML & Markdown
hero.demoQuestion: What notice period applies to termination for convenience?
hero.demoAnswerTail: , and prepaid fees are refunded pro-rata
hero.demoSourceQuote: “…terminate upon thirty (30) days’ prior written notice…”

stats.supportedLabel: of citations rated supported by the auditor
stats.latencyLabel: from clicking a citation to the highlight

how.title: From document to defensible answer.
how.subtitle: Four steps, no setup. Upload your sources and start asking — every answer arrives ready to verify.
how.step1Body: Drag in PDFs, DOCX, HTML or Markdown. Cite extracts, chunks and indexes them automatically.
how.step2Body: Cite classifies the question, breaks it into sub-queries and runs hybrid search with reranking.
how.step3Body: The answer streams in with inline citations — each one a clickable link to its grounding passage.
how.step4Body: Open the source, see the exact highlight, and check the auditor's verdict. Trust, then cite.

retrieval.title: Retrieval you can watch think.
retrieval.body: No black-box "thinking…" spinner. Cite shows each phase — classify, decompose, search, rerank, check sufficiency — and lets you stop anytime.
retrieval.point1: Hybrid vector + keyword search with reranking
retrieval.point3: Sufficiency check before it answers — or says it can't

viewer.title: Click a citation. See the exact passage.
viewer.body: Every citation opens the source in a side viewer, scrolled to the precise spot — a bounding box on a PDF page or a text range in HTML, highlighted in evidence amber.
viewer.point1: PDF bounding boxes & HTML text ranges
viewer.point2: Select any text to leave a region comment
viewer.point3: Resizable split that snaps back when you're done
viewer.docClause2: Material breach uncured thirty days after notice.
viewer.docHighlightBody: Either party may terminate for any reason upon thirty (30) days' prior written notice.

audit.title: Every citation, scored.
audit.bodyLead: An automated judge rates each citation
audit.bodyTail: , with a confidence score and short reasoning. Admins get a dashboard to triage the weak ones.
audit.point2: Filter to unsupported claims, jump to context
audit.point3: Per-citation reasoning, not just a number
audit.cardReason: "Refunded on a pro-rated basis" — source omits the method.

collab.title: Decide together, in the evidence.
collab.body: Comment on a message or a document region, reply, resolve — and see who else is in the room in real time. The whole team works over the same source of truth.
collab.demoMessage: Either party may terminate on 30 days' notice
collab.demoCommentBody: Flag 30-day notice as the controlling provision?

features.title: A complete evidence workspace.
features.inlineBody: Numbered chips in every answer, each a link to its grounding passage.
features.highlightBody: Exact bounding boxes on PDFs and text ranges in HTML & Markdown.
features.auditBody: An LLM judge scores every citation with confidence and reasoning.
features.retrievalBody: Vector + keyword search with reranking and sub-query decomposition.
features.collabBody: Presence, live sync and threaded comments on messages and regions.
features.workspacesBody: Multi-tenant orgs with admin / member roles and shareable invites.

useCases.title: For teams who have to be right.
useCases.subtitle: Anywhere a wrong answer is expensive, the citation is the deliverable.
useCases.legalBody: Pin the controlling clause across MSAs and amendments — with the page and line to back it.
useCases.complianceBody: Answer audits and regulators with a defensible trail from claim to source policy.
useCases.supportBody: Resolve from runbooks with the exact step cited — no more guessing at stale docs.
useCases.researchBody: Synthesize across dense reports and trace every figure back to its primary source.

faq.title: Good to know.
faq.q1: What file formats can I upload?
faq.a1: PDF, DOCX, HTML and Markdown — up to 100 MB each. Cite extracts, chunks, embeds and indexes them automatically, then shows the ingestion status per document.
faq.a2: An automated judge compares each cited claim against its source passage and labels it Supported, Partial or Unsupported, with a confidence score and short reasoning you can review.
faq.q3: Does it work with my own private documents?
faq.a3: Yes. Documents stay inside your organization's workspace and are visible only to members according to their roles. We never sell your data or train third-party models on it.
faq.q4: Can my team collaborate in real time?
faq.a4: Live presence, real-time message sync and threaded comments on messages and document regions keep your whole team working over one source of truth.
faq.a5: Yes. The entire interface is available in English and Brazilian Portuguese, and you can switch language at any time.

cta.title: Stop trusting. Start verifying.
cta.subtitle: Create a workspace, upload your first source, and get a cited answer in minutes.

footer.tagline: Agentic answers with verifiable citations. The evidence workspace for teams who must defend their answers.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This Privacy Policy explains how Cite collects, uses, and protects information when you use the Service. Your documents are yours — we treat them as confidential and never sell your data.
privacy.s1Body: We collect account details (name, email), the documents and content you upload, the questions and conversations you create, and usage and device information needed to operate and secure the Service.
privacy.s2Body: We use information to provide the Service — indexing your sources, generating cited answers, auditing citations, and enabling collaboration — and to maintain security, prevent abuse, and improve reliability. We do not use Your Content to train third-party models without your instruction.
privacy.s3Body: Documents you upload are stored within your organization's workspace and are accessible only to its members according to their roles. Your workspace is yours: you can export or delete your content and your account at any time.
privacy.s4Body: We share information only with service providers ("subprocessors") that help us run the Service — such as cloud hosting and model providers — under contracts that require appropriate confidentiality and security. We do not sell personal information or document content.
privacy.s5Body: We protect data in transit and at rest with encryption, enforce role-based access within workspaces, and apply administrative and technical safeguards aligned with industry best practices. No method of transmission or storage is perfectly secure, but we work continuously to protect your information and to notify you of incidents where required.
privacy.s6Body: We retain information for as long as your account is active or as needed to provide the Service. When you delete content or close your account, we delete or de-identify the associated data within a reasonable period, except where retention is required by law.
privacy.s7Body: Depending on your location, you may have rights to access, correct, export, or delete your personal data, and to object to or restrict certain processing (for example under the LGPD or GDPR). You can exercise many of these directly in-product, or by contacting us.
privacy.s8Body: We use essential cookies to keep you signed in and to remember your preferences such as theme and language. Where data is transferred across borders, we use appropriate safeguards consistent with applicable law.
privacy.s9BodyLead: We may update this Policy; material changes will be communicated in-product or by email. For privacy questions or to reach our Data Protection Officer, contact

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.intro: These Terms of Use ("Terms") govern your access to and use of Cite, an evidence workspace for grounded answers with verifiable citations (the "Service"), provided by Cite. By creating an account or using the Service, you agree to these Terms.
terms.s1Body: Cite lets teams upload documents into collections and ask questions in natural language. Answers are generated with inline citations linked to the source passages, and citations are automatically audited for accuracy. Features may change over time as we improve the Service.
terms.s2Body: You must provide accurate account information and keep your credentials secure. You are responsible for activity under your account and for your workspace members. Admins may manage members, roles, and invitations within their organization.
terms.s3Body: You agree not to misuse the Service: no unlawful content, no infringement of others' rights, no attempts to disrupt or reverse-engineer the Service, and no uploading of material you do not have the right to use. We may suspend accounts that violate these Terms.
terms.s4Body: You retain ownership of the documents and content you upload ("Your Content"). You grant Cite a limited licence to host, process, index, and display Your Content solely to provide the Service to you and your workspace. You are responsible for having the rights to upload and use Your Content.
terms.s5Body: Answers are generated automatically and, while grounded in your sources and auditable, may contain errors. Citation verdicts are an automated quality signal, not a guarantee. You are responsible for reviewing answers and citations before relying on them, particularly for legal, compliance, or other high-stakes decisions.
terms.s6Body: The Service, including its software, design, and trademarks, is owned by Cite and protected by intellectual-property laws. These Terms do not grant you any rights in the Service except the limited right to use it as described.
terms.s7BodyLead: We apply administrative, technical, and organizational safeguards to protect Your Content, as described in our
terms.s7BodyTail: . We do not sell Your Content and do not use it to train third-party models without your instruction.
terms.s8Body: The Service is provided "as is" and "as available" without warranties of any kind, whether express or implied, including fitness for a particular purpose and non-infringement, to the maximum extent permitted by law.
terms.s9Body: To the maximum extent permitted by law, Cite will not be liable for indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages, or for lost profits or data, arising from your use of the Service. Our aggregate liability is limited to the amounts you paid for the Service in the twelve months preceding the claim.
terms.s10Body: You may stop using the Service at any time. We may suspend or terminate access if you breach these Terms or to protect the Service. On termination, your right to use the Service ends; we will make Your Content available for export for a reasonable period unless prohibited by law.
terms.s11Body: We may update these Terms; material changes will be communicated in-product or by email, and continued use constitutes acceptance. These Terms are governed by the laws of the jurisdiction in which Cite is established, without regard to conflict-of-laws principles.
terms.s12BodyLead: Questions about these Terms? Reach us at
